name: My Steak and Kidney Pudding
recipeIngredient: Beef stock OR Bovril and water OR Water
recipeInstructions:
1. Blend pastry ingredients: flour, baking powder, salt, and suet in a processor. Process until mealy.
2. Add cold water while running the processor to form a lump.
3. Roll out the dough and line a 3-quart bowl with it, pressing flat against the sides. Leave at least 1 inch hanging over the sides all around. Use extra pastry for the top.
4. Dice the meat and kidneys. Coat them well with flour by mixing seasonings (salt and pepper) with the flour and sprinkling about 1 tablespoon in the bottom of the pudding.
5. Add a layer of meat, sprinkle more flour mixture on top, then repeat until all the meat and kidneys are used.
6. Finish with another layer of flour mixture.
7. Cover the filling with enough stock, water, or Bovril and water to fill the bowl. Roll extra pastry to make a lid and place it on top of the filling.
8. Fold the edges of the pastry lining over the lid of the pastry.
9. Make a small hole in the middle of the lid for adding more liquid if needed.
10. Cover the bowl with a large dish towel, then cover the top with foil and bring the corners up to tie two at a time.
11. Place the bowl in a steamer over boiling water and keep boiling hard for 2 hours.
12. Reduce heat and simmer for 2 or more hours longer, refilling water as needed.
13. When cooked, remove from the steamer and serve with your choice of vegetables. To thicken gravy, pour a little more stock into the hole in the pastry lid.
